attacking_style: Not a primary attacker; relies on late-race sprint positioning within final 2km. Depends on organized leadout delivery rather than initiating moves.
team_tactics: Quick-Step provides flexible support but he holds secondary status in hierarchy. Team will abandon sprint prep if race situation demands different tactics. Leadout releases him around 1600m mark.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where positioning and organization matter more than pure power. Messy, disorganized finales appear to favor his opportunistic style over structured sprints.
vulnerability: Exposed when team releases leadout too early (before 1600m) for his sprint characteristics. Sacrificed when Quick-Step has conflicting GC or tactical priorities in split race situations.
key_patterns: Secondary status within Quick-Step hierarchy; twice abandoned or deprioritized when team had other options.
Succeeds in chaotic finales when opponent trains disintegrate; won when Bennett's leadout fell apart.
Timing-sensitive sprinter requiring late release; struggled when deployed before 1600m mark.
Benefits from team flexibility; won when Quick-Step unexpectedly pivoted to support him over GC.
form_trajectory: neutral: Mixed results showing capability to win when supported, but unclear development trajectory given secondary team role
